name: Red and Black Raspberry Pudding Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Thaw and drain the frozen red raspberries well, reserving about one-third of the syrup. Pick over the fresh berries.
2. In a heavy saucepan, combine the sugar and cornstarch. Add the reserved syrup and lemon juice to the mixture, stirring until well combined.
3. Add the thawed fruit and fresh black raspberries to the saucepan. Bring the mixture to a boil over moderate heat, stirring constantly, then simmer for 5 minutes.
4. In a large bowl, cream the butter until smooth. Gradually add the sugar, beating continuously until light and fluffy.
5. Add the egg and vanilla; beat until smooth.
6. Sift together the flour, baking powder, and salt into another bowl.
7. Alternately add the flour mixture and milk to the butter mixture in small batches, mixing well after each addition until a smooth batter forms.
8. Pour the batter evenly into a lightly buttered 9-inch square baking pan. Spoon the berry mixture over the top of the batter.
9. Bake in the center of a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 20 minutes. Reduce the temperature to 325°F (160°C) and bake for an additional 20-25 minutes, or until a tester inserted into the center comes out clean.
10. Allow the cake to cool on a rack for 10 minutes before serving with cream or whipped cream.
